pi_items.source: setup_drupal_commerce.sh line 249 creates user 'mikewilson' with email mike.wilson@example.com. Confirmed in verifier.py and setup_task.sh.
Task description explicitly requires creating a new Order Type named 'Wholesale'. Confirmed in verifier.py which searches for 'wholesale' in order type labels.

Drupal Commerce documentation (https://docs.drupalcommerce.org/v2/developer-guide/orders/workflows/) confirms 'order_fulfillment' is the workflow ID for the Fulfillment workflow. Task requires Fulfillment workflow (not default).
Task description explicitly requires creating a new Order Item Type named 'Wholesale Product' that uses 'Product variation' as purchasable entity.

privileged_info_summary: The task configures a Drupal Commerce B2B environment. It requires creating a 'Wholesale' order type using the 'order_fulfillment' workflow, creating a 'Wholesale Product' order item type linked to product variations, and creating a draft order for the pre-existing customer 'mikewilson'. All user and product data are synthetic, created by setup scripts for testing. The workflow ID 'order_fulfillment' is the standard Drupal Commerce identifier for the Fulfillment workflow which supports order states: Draft, Fulfillment, Completed, and Canceled.
